rules.rule: Foundation models MUST represent domain or API-facing concepts.
View models MUST represent UI-ready data produced by view services.
Component prop models MUST describe rendering input only.
Do NOT reuse API response models directly as component props when the UI needs a different shape.
Models MUST NOT contain behavior (methods, functions, API calls, mutations).
Models MUST be organized by architectural layer: foundations/, views/, components/, configurations/.
Use `type` keyword for model definitions (not `interface` unless defining a behavioral contract).
Model files MUST follow pattern `{Domain}.ts` or `{Domain}{Purpose}.ts`.
Foundation models SHOULD match backend domain models when possible.
View models MUST be transformation output from view services, never created in components.
Optional properties MUST use `?` only when the field can truly be absent.
Use explicit types for all properties — never implicit `any`.
Prefer explicit union types over string for fixed sets of values.
Date/time values SHOULD be strings (ISO 8601) at API boundaries, Date objects internally when needed.
Avoid deeply nested model structures beyond 3 levels.
Error models MUST be separate from domain models.
